description: Testes de validacao v3 com dados reais 141Air janeiro e fevereiro 2026. Garantir que o event ledger (26 event types) produz os mesmos resultados que o sistema anterior para despesas, cash events e DRE.

userStories.title: Expense classifier end-to-end test with real non-order payments
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to validate that classify_non_order_payment() produces correct expense_captured and expense_classified events using real 141air payment data.
acceptanceCriteria: File testes/test_v3_validation_141air.py created
Test loads real payments from testes/data/cache_jan2026/141air_payments.json
Filters non-order payments (no order_id, or order_id but filtered by processor rules)
For each non-order payment: calls classify_non_order_payment() with mocked DB
Verifies record_expense_event() was called with event_type='expense_captured' for each
Verifies signed_amount sign: negative for 'expense' direction, positive for 'income'
Verifies competencia_date extracted from date_approved (not date_created)
Verifies metadata contains all required fields: expense_type, expense_direction, ca_category, auto_categorized, description, amount
If auto_categorized: verifies expense_classified event also recorded
Counts match: total non-order payments == total expense_captured calls
Same test repeated with testes/data/cache_fev2026/141air_payments.json
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read app/services/expense_classifier.py fully to understand classify_non_order_payment(). Read testes/test_dre_reconciliation.py for fixture patterns (loading cache, filtering). The non-order filter is: payments where order_id is None or empty, PLUS payments excluded by processor filters (marketplace_shipment, collector_id match, etc). Read testes/test_expense_classifier_dual_write.py for the mock pattern (mock db.table, mock record_expense_event). IMPORTANT: after Etapa 5, there is NO dual-write — classify_non_order_payment only calls record_expense_event, NOT db.table('mp_expenses').

userStories.title: Extrato ingestion validation test with real CSV — jan + fev 141Air
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to validate that extrato classification produces correct results from real 141Air extrato CSVs for both January and February 2026.
acceptanceCriteria: Test class TestExtratoIngestionJan2026 in test_v3_validation_141air.py
Parses real extrato CSV: testes/data/extratos/extrato janeiro 141Air.csv
Uses _parse_account_statement() and _classify_extrato_line() from extrato_ingester.py
For lines classified as expenses (not skip, not _CHECK_PAYMENTS): verifies the classification produces valid expense_type and ca_category
Counts: total lines parsed == 690 (January 141Air known count)
Classification breakdown matches known distribution: ~50 skips, ~330 payment_events, ~310 expenses
Verifies _EXPENSE_TYPE_ABBREV has an entry for every expense_type that appears in real data
Verifies _CA_CATEGORY_CODE_MAP has an entry for every ca_category that appears in real data
Same validation for February CSV: testes/data/extratos/extrato fevereiro 141Air.csv
February line count validated
Zero unclassified lines in both months (every line gets a classification or is a skip)
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read app/services/extrato_ingester.py — key functions: _parse_account_statement() (line ~250), _classify_extrato_line() (line ~335), EXTRATO_CLASSIFICATION_RULES (line ~88), _CHECK_PAYMENTS (line ~151), _EXPENSE_TYPE_ABBREV (line ~163). Read testes/test_extrato_classification.py for the existing pattern — it already tests classification. The test should be OFFLINE — no Supabase calls, just parsing + classification logic.

userStories.title: DRE integrity: v3 event types produce identical DRE to v2 reference
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm that the v3 event ledger with 26 event types produces the exact same DRE totals as the v2 reference values, with cash and expense events properly excluded.
acceptanceCriteria: Test class TestDREIntegrityV3 in test_v3_validation_141air.py
Processes all 141air jan2026 payments through processor math (_extract_processor_charges, _to_float, _to_brt_date)
Simulates event creation for all 16 payment event types + 6 cash + 4 expense
Validates all events via validate_event() — no validation errors for any of the 26 types
Builds DRE from simulated events grouping by event_type
EXPLICITLY verifies cash_* and expense_* events are EXCLUDED from DRE calculation (simulates get_dre_summary filter)
January reference values match exactly: receita_bruta=179572.25, comissao=23085.97, frete=8946.37, devolucoes=45375.41, resultado=109555.37
Same validation for February with known fev2026 reference values from test_dre_reconciliation_fev2026.py
Verifies EVENT_TYPES dict has exactly 26 entries
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read testes/test_dre_reconciliation.py for January reference values and fixture pattern. Read testes/test_dre_reconciliation_fev2026.py for February. Read app/services/event_ledger.py get_dre_summary() to see the filter that excludes cash_* and expense_* events. The CRITICAL v3 guarantee is: adding 10 new event types (4 expense + 6 cash) does NOT change DRE numbers. This test proves that.

userStories.title: Cash event mapping completeness with real extrato — sum invariant
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to validate that every extrato line maps to a cash event type and that the sum of all cash event amounts equals the extrato net movement.
acceptanceCriteria: Test class TestCashEventMapping in test_v3_validation_141air.py
Loads and parses January 141Air extrato CSV (690 lines)
For each line: determines the cash event type using the same classification logic as ingest_extrato_to_ledger.py (CASH_TYPE_MAP, SKIP_TO_CASH_TYPE)
Every line produces exactly one cash event type (no unmapped lines)
Cash event type distribution has no zeros — every mapped type appears at least once
Sum invariant: sum of all 690 signed amounts == -3385.83 (known January net: 4476.23 initial - 1090.40 final)
No cash event type is 'unknown' or None
Same validation for February extrato CSV with February's known initial/final balance
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read testes/ingest_extrato_to_ledger.py for CASH_TYPE_MAP, SKIP_TO_CASH_TYPE, SKIP_ABBREV — these are the authoritative mappings. Read app/services/extrato_ingester.py for _parse_account_statement() and _classify_extrato_line(). The sum invariant is the KEY test: initial_balance + sum(all_amounts) must equal final_balance. For January: 4476.23 + (-3385.83) = 1090.40. Import and reuse the mappings from ingest_extrato_to_ledger.py, do NOT reimplement.

userStories.title: Event ledger contract tests: idempotency keys, metadata, filters
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to validate record_expense_event(), record_cash_event(), and derive_expense_status() contracts with representative real-world inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Test class TestEventLedgerContracts in test_v3_validation_141air.py
Tests record_expense_event() idempotency key format: {seller}:{payment_id}:{event_type} (3 parts)
Tests record_cash_event() idempotency key format: {seller}:{ref_id}:{event_type}:{date}:{abbrev} (5 parts)
Tests record_cash_event() rejects event_type not starting with 'cash_'
Tests record_cash_event() sets competencia_date == event_date
Tests derive_expense_status() all 5 cases: empty->unknown, captured->pending_review, classified->auto_categorized, reviewed->reviewed, exported->exported
Tests derive_expense_status() priority: exported wins over reviewed wins over classified
Tests validate_event() accepts all 26 event types with correct sign constraints
Tests validate_event() rejects wrong signs: positive amount for negative-only type, etc
All tests use mocked Supabase (offline)
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read app/services/event_ledger.py for: record_expense_event(), record_cash_event(), derive_expense_status(), validate_event(), EVENT_TYPES dict. Read testes/test_event_ledger.py and testes/test_cash_events.py for existing mock patterns. Some of these tests may overlap with existing ones — check first and only add what's MISSING. If a test already exists in another file, add a comment referencing it instead of duplicating.
